idea.hook: Think light speed is instant? Not across the cosmos.
idea.payoff: Even at light speed, reaching the Andromeda Galaxy would take a mind-boggling 2.5 million years!
idea.title: Light Speed Journey Times! 🤯🚀

scenes.text: LIGHT SPEED: Not as instant as you think...
scenes.visual: A sleek, stylized spacecraft streaks across a vibrant starfield, then visually 'drags' or leaves a very long, glowing trail behind it to emphasize the vastness and time involved. Background is deep space with distant, twinkling stars.
A realistic visualization of Earth and its Moon. A glowing light beam quickly travels from Earth to the Moon, highlighting the short time. The text appears clearly above the planets.

A dynamic shot showing Earth and Mars, further apart. A light path is depicted, moving more slowly or with a progress bar-like animation, emphasizing the increased duration. A small digital clock visual could briefly animate the minutes ticking by.

Our Sun (as a small star) and Proxima Centauri (as a slightly larger, more prominent star) against a backdrop of deep space, showing immense void. A faint, long, dashed light-path stretches between them, fading into the distance to illustrate the colossal distance and time.

A breathtaking, wide shot of the Milky Way and Andromeda galaxies, slowly pulling apart or rotating to emphasize the mind-boggling intergalactic distance. The text is very prominent, with 'MILLION years' perhaps animating with a slight color change or larger size for impact.
